description_long: This object is a classic regulatory stop sign, featuring a red octagonal face with a bold white border and the word 'STOP' in large, uppercase white letters at its center. The sign is mounted on a slender, metallic pole that is bent at an angle, suggesting it may have been displaced or is not currently installed upright. The pole appears to be made of galvanized steel, providing durability and resistance to weather. The sign face is likely constructed from aluminum with a reflective coating to enhance visibility at night or in low-light conditions. The rear of the sign is plain and unmarked, showing the metallic support structure. The overall design is utilitarian, intended for clear visibility and communication in urban traffic environments.
description: A red octagonal stop sign with white border and 'STOP' text, mounted on a bent metallic pole. The sign is highly visible and designed for urban traffic control.
description_view_0: The front view shows the red octagonal sign face with white 'STOP' text and border, mounted on a bent pole.
description_view_1: A side profile reveals the thinness of the sign and the straightness of the pole from this angle.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the plain back of the sign and the full length of the bent pole.
description_view_3: Another side profile, similar to view 1, emphasizing the sign's slim profile and the pole's bend.
